_meta.description: HR login demo data. Evergreen dates: weekly meetings by dayOfWeek, monthly meetings by dayOfMonth; tasks by dueOffsetDays; emails by receivedMinutesAgo. Includes per-persona activity grid (day x hour), workWeek location, and loggedTodayMinutes for the time tracker.

tasks.title: Approve Q2 report
tasks.description: Approve Q2 attrition report for board

emails.subject: Re: Q2 headcount & cost forecast
emails.preview: Can you confirm the contractor-to-FTE ratio before the board meeting?
emails.body: Hi Sarah, before the board meeting I want to align on the contractor-to-FTE ratio. The forecast shows contractor spend up 8% versus Q1 — can you confirm whether that's driven by the Production backfills? Happy to jump on a quick call this afternoon if that's easier.

emails.subject: Attrition deep-dive: Production unit
emails.preview: Sharing the first-year churn breakdown you asked for.
emails.body: Sarah, sharing the first-year churn breakdown you asked for. The Production cluster is the main driver — voluntary exits inside the first 12 months are running well above the company average, and work-life balance comes up repeatedly in exit interviews. Full numbers attached; I've flagged the regrettable cases.

emails.subject: Agenda — Board meeting 26 June
emails.preview: Your workforce update is item 4 on the final agenda.
emails.body: Dear Sarah, please find the final agenda for the board meeting on 26 June. Your workforce update is item 4, with a 15-minute slot including Q&A. Kindly send any supporting slides by end of Wednesday so they can be circulated in advance.

emails.subject: 2026 training plan — ready for approval
emails.preview: Finalised with completion targets per program.
emails.body: Hi Sarah, the 2026 L&D plan is finalised with completion targets per program and a revised budget. Headline: we're shifting more spend toward internal leadership development based on this year's outcome data. Ready for your approval whenever you have a moment.

emails.subject: Workforce cost review — Q3 planning
emails.preview: Need your headcount assumptions for the Q3 reforecast.
emails.body: Hi Sarah, we're kicking off the Q3 reforecast and I need your latest headcount and contractor assumptions. Could you send the working numbers by Thursday so Finance can model the cost lines?

emails.subject: Board pack — attrition narrative
emails.preview: Could you add a short commentary on the regrettable-attrition trend?
emails.body: Sarah, the board pack is nearly ready. Could you add two or three lines on the regrettable-attrition trend and what we're doing about first-year churn? Keep it high-level.

emails.subject: Leadership programme — extra seats
emails.preview: The Leadership track is oversubscribed; can we approve 6 more seats?
emails.body: The Leadership development track filled up faster than expected. I've got six strong people on the waitlist — are you comfortable approving the extra seats within the existing budget?

emails.subject: Engagement pulse results are in
emails.preview: Overall engagement ticked up; Sales still lags the company average.
emails.body: This period's pulse is ready. Overall engagement improved versus last quarter, though Sales continues to sit below the company average. Full breakdown by department is in the dashboard.

tasks.title: Update job grades
tasks.description: Update job architecture for Sales division

tasks.title: Approve leave
tasks.description: Approve July leave requests for BU

emails.subject: Re: Attrition deep-dive: Production unit
emails.preview: Can you add the regrettable split before Wednesday?
emails.body: Thanks David, this is exactly what I needed. Can you add the regrettable-vs-non-regrettable split before Wednesday's review? I'd like to show the board we're losing people we wanted to keep, not just managing natural turnover.

emails.subject: Concern about team workload
emails.preview: Two of my technicians have flagged burnout risk.
emails.body: David, two of my technicians have flagged they're close to burnout after last month's overtime push. I don't want to lose them — can we look at temporary backfill or redistributing the shift load? Would appreciate your advice on the right way to handle this.

emails.subject: Headcount data refresh complete
emails.preview: The Q2 master file is updated and ready.
emails.body: Hi David, the Q2 master headcount file is refreshed and reconciled — 2,845 records across all departments. You're clear to pull from it for the attrition deep-dive. Let me know if any figures look off.

emails.subject: Engagement results are ready
emails.preview: Your team's latest engagement scores are available.
emails.body: Your team's latest engagement results are now available in the dashboard. The overall score is steady, but the Production sub-team dipped on 'manager support'. Log in to view the full breakdown by question and team.

emails.subject: Cover for two long-term absences
emails.preview: Two of my team are on extended leave — can we arrange temporary cover?
emails.body: Hi David, I now have two people on extended leave in Production. Can we look at temporary cover or a short-term contractor so the line isn't understaffed through the summer?

emails.subject: Performance conversation — guidance
emails.preview: I'd like your steer before a difficult review.
emails.body: David, I've got a tricky performance conversation coming up and want to handle it well. Could we grab 20 minutes so you can steer me on tone and what to document?

emails.subject: Return-to-work interviews overdue
emails.preview: Two return-to-work interviews are overdue in your queue.
emails.body: A reminder that two return-to-work interviews are past their due date in your queue. Completing them keeps the absence records compliant — let us know if you need the templates.

emails.subject: Flexible working request
emails.preview: I'd like to discuss moving to a 4-day week.
emails.body: Hi David, I'd like to explore moving to a four-day week from next quarter. Could we set up a short chat to talk through how it would work for my role and team?

tasks.title: Schedule interviews
tasks.description: Schedule final interviews for Data Analyst role

tasks.title: Send offer
tasks.description: Send offer to Production Technician candidate

tasks.title: Post 3 roles
tasks.description: Post 3 open roles to job boards

emails.subject: Re: Data Analyst — interview availability
emails.preview: I'm available Thursday afternoon or Friday morning.
emails.body: Hi Aisha, thank you for the update — I'm really excited about the role. I'm available Thursday afternoon or Friday morning for the final round. Let me know what suits the panel and I'll make it work.

emails.subject: Feedback on shortlist
emails.preview: Let's move forward with candidates 1 and 3.
emails.body: Hi Aisha, I've reviewed the three CVs for the Data Analyst position. Let's move forward with candidates 1 and 3 — both have the SQL and dashboarding depth we need. Candidate 2 was strong but less of a team fit. Can we get them in this week?

emails.subject: 5 new applicants for your job post
emails.preview: New applicants for Production Technician I.
emails.body: You have 5 new applicants for your job post 'Production Technician I'. Two match more than 80% of your listed criteria. Review and respond promptly to keep candidates engaged.

emails.subject: Pipeline update for Thursday 1:1
emails.preview: Can you bring time-to-fill numbers to our 1:1?
emails.body: Aisha, looking forward to our 1:1 on Thursday. Could you bring the latest time-to-fill numbers and the status of the two senior roles? The board will ask about recruitment velocity and I want us aligned.

emails.subject: Data Analyst shortlist
emails.preview: Can we book the final three for panels this week?
emails.body: Hi Aisha, thanks for the shortlist. The final three look strong — can we get them booked for panel interviews this week before they go cold? I can flex my calendar.

emails.subject: Two senior engineers — profiles attached
emails.preview: Sharing two strong profiles for the platform team.
emails.body: Hi Aisha, sharing two senior engineer profiles we think fit the platform role well. Both are available to interview next week — happy to arrange intro calls if useful.

emails.subject: Re: Offer — a couple of questions
emails.preview: Thanks for the offer! Could you clarify relocation support?
emails.body: Hi Aisha, thank you so much for the offer — I'm excited. Before I sign, could you clarify the relocation support and the start-date flexibility? Then I'm ready to go.

emails.subject: New starter checklist — 3 pending
emails.preview: Three starters begin Monday; equipment requests still open.
emails.body: Three new starters begin Monday and their equipment requests are still open. Could you nudge IT so laptops and accounts are ready for day one?

tasks.title: Book trainer
tasks.description: Book trainer for Leadership Development cohort

tasks.title: Review completions
tasks.description: Review completion rates: Customer Service program

tasks.title: Prep impact slide
tasks.description: Prepare training-impact slide for board deck

emails.subject: Re: 2026 training plan
emails.preview: Approving today. Add completion targets to the board slide.
emails.body: Looks good, Lucas — approving today. One ask: add the completion-rate targets to the board slide so leadership can see the link between training spend and outcomes. Great work pulling this together.

emails.subject: Invoice & June cohort confirmation
emails.preview: Confirming your Leadership Development cohort for next month.
emails.body: Dear Lucas, this confirms your Leadership Development cohort scheduled for next month (12 participants). The invoice is attached and due in 30 days. Please confirm the final attendee list one week before the start date.

emails.subject: 14 employees overdue on mandatory training
emails.preview: The following employees have not completed required modules.
emails.body: Automated notice: 14 employees have not completed their mandatory training modules by the due date. The list is attached and managers have been copied. Please follow up to ensure compliance before quarter-end.

emails.subject: Training cost reconciliation Q2
emails.preview: The training spend vs budget reconciliation is attached.
emails.body: Hi Lucas, the Q2 training spend vs budget reconciliation is attached. We're tracking slightly under budget overall, but the external-vendor line is over — likely the SkillForge invoice. Can you confirm the coding so I can close the quarter?

emails.subject: Q3 leadership cohort — scheduling
emails.preview: Proposing dates for the next leadership cohort; which suits?
emails.body: Hi Lucas, we're ready to schedule the Q3 leadership cohort. I've proposed three date options in the attachment — let me know which works and we'll lock the trainers.

emails.subject: Training budget reallocation
emails.preview: Can we shift underused Communication budget to Technical?
emails.body: Lucas, the Communication programme is tracking under budget while Technical is oversubscribed. Are you okay to reallocate the difference? I'll process it once you confirm.

emails.subject: Certification renewals due
emails.preview: Four of my engineers have certs expiring next month.
emails.body: Hi Lucas, four of my engineers have certifications expiring next month. Can we get them into a renewal cohort so there's no lapse? Happy to prioritise whoever you suggest.

emails.subject: Course completion dip in June
emails.preview: Completion rates dropped last month; summary attached.
emails.body: June course completion dropped compared with May, mostly on self-paced modules. The summary is attached — you may want to send a gentle nudge to the lagging teams.

tasks.title: Reconcile spend
tasks.description: Reconcile training spend vs budget Q2

emails.subject: June payroll cut-off reminder
emails.preview: Please submit final payroll changes by Thursday 17:00.
emails.body: Reminder: the cut-off for June payroll changes is Thursday at 17:00. Please submit all new starters, leavers, and salary adjustments before then. Anything received after the deadline will roll into the July run.

emails.subject: New starters for July — details attached
emails.preview: Here are the 4 new contracts for onboarding setup.
emails.body: Hi Emma, attached are the four signed contracts for July onboarding — two Production, one Sales, one Finance. Can you set up their payroll and system access? Start date is the 1st for all four.

emails.subject: Re: Training cost reconciliation Q2
emails.preview: The SkillForge invoice should be coded to L&D.
emails.body: Thanks Emma — the SkillForge invoice should be coded to the L&D external-vendor line, not general training. That should explain the overage. Let me know if you need anything else to close Q2.

emails.subject: Q2 contribution statement
emails.preview: Your quarterly contribution statement is now available.
emails.body: Your quarterly pension contribution statement is now available. Total contributions and any adjustments are detailed in the attached document. Please review and confirm before the 30th.

emails.subject: July run — cut-off reminder
emails.preview: Please submit variable inputs by the 20th.
emails.body: Hi Emma, a reminder that variable pay inputs for the July run are due by the 20th. Anything received after that moves to the August run — let us know if you need an extension.

emails.subject: Payslip query — overtime
emails.preview: My June payslip seems to miss two overtime shifts.
emails.body: Hi Emma, I think my June payslip is missing two overtime shifts from the end of the month. Could you check against my logged hours? Happy to send the dates.

emails.subject: Pension provider switch — action
emails.preview: We need updated enrolment data for the new provider.
emails.body: Emma, the pension provider switch goes live next month. We need the updated enrolment file from your side to avoid gaps — can you send it by Friday?

emails.subject: Two contracts expiring
emails.preview: Can you confirm renewal or offboarding for two fixed-term staff?
emails.body: Hi Emma, two of my fixed-term team members have contracts expiring in three weeks. Could you confirm whether we're renewing or offboarding so I can plan cover?
